forecasts.reasoning: Han Duck-soo, at 75 years old, is a career technocrat currently serving as Acting President. South Korean history shows a strong precedent of acting presidents (e.g., Goh Kun, Hwang Kyo-ahn) declining to run in the subsequent snap elections to maintain political neutrality and manage the transition crisis, despite calls from their parties to run. Furthermore, running would require him to resign as acting president, creating further instability by passing the role to the Deputy Prime Minister. Given this context and the usual reluctance of career bureaucrats to enter the partisan electoral fray, the probability of him announcing a presidential bid by May 5 is low.
